name: pyln-testing

metadata.name: pyln-testing
metadata.summary: Test your Core Lightning integration, plugins or whatever you want

documentation:
# pyln-testing: A library to write tests against Core Lightning

This library implements a number of utilities that help building tests for
Core Lightning nodes. In particular it provides a number of pytest fixtures that
allow the management of a test network of a given topology and then execute a
test scenarion.

`pyln-testing` is used by Core Lightning for its internal tests, and by the
community plugin directory to exercise the plugins.

## Installation

`pyln-testing` is available on `pip`:

```bash
pip install pyln-testing
```

Alternatively you can also install the development version to get access to
currently unreleased features by checking out the Core Lightning source code and
installing into your python3 environment:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ElementsProject/lightning.git
cd lightning/contrib/pyln-testing
poetry install
```

This will add links to the library into your environment so changing the
checked out source code will also result in the environment picking up these
changes. Notice however that unreleased versions may change API without
warning, so test thoroughly with the released version.

## Testing GRPC Bindings

The grpc bindings can be tested by setting the `CLN_TEST_GRPC=1`
environment variable. This will cause the testing framework to use a
grpc client to talk to the `cln-grpc` plugin, rather than talking
directly to the node's JSON-RPC interface. Since the GRPC related
dependencies are guarded behind a feature flag in `pyln-testing`
you'll need to install it with the `grpc` feature enabled in order to
be able to run in this mode.

Below is a diagram of how the normal JSON-RPC interaction looks like,
followed by one that display the grpc interaction:

```
CLN -- JSON-RPC -- LightningRpc -- pytest
\_____CLN_____/    \_______pytest_______/
```

```
CLN -- JSON-RPC -- cln-rpc -- rpc2grpc converters -- grpc interface -- python grpc client -- python grpc2json converter -- pytest
\_____CLN_____/    \___________cln-grpc-plugin____________________/    \__________________________pytest________________________/
```

As you can see the grpc mode attempts to emulate the simple JSON-RPC
mode by passing the call through a number of conversions. The last
step `grpc2json` is rather incomplete, and will cause quite a few
tests to fail for now, until the conversion is completed and we reach
feature parity between the interaction modes.

modules.name: pyln.testing.btcproxy
documentation: A bitcoind proxy that allows instrumentation and canned responses

classes.name: pyln.testing.btcproxy.DecimalEncoder
documentation: By default json.dumps does not handle Decimals correctly, so we override it's handling

methods.name: pyln.testing.btcproxy.BitcoinRpcProxy.mock_rpc
documentation:
Mock the response to a future RPC call of @method

The response can either be a dict with the full JSON-RPC response, or a
function that returns such a response. If the response is None the mock
is removed and future calls will be passed through to bitcoind again.

modules.name: pyln.testing.grpc
documentation: A drop-in replacement for the JSON-RPC LightningRpc

methods.name: pyln.testing.grpc.LightningGrpc.connect
documentation: Connect to {peer_id} at {host} and {port}.

methods.name: pyln.testing.grpc.LightningGrpc.getpeer
documentation: Show peer with {peer_id}, if {level} is set, include {log}s.

methods.name: pyln.testing.grpc.LightningGrpc.newaddr
documentation: Get a new address of type {addresstype} of the internal wallet.

methods.name: pyln.testing.db.Sqlite3Db.get_dsn
documentation: SQLite3 doesn't provide a DSN, resulting in no CLI-option.

methods.name: pyln.testing.db.PostgresDb.stop
documentation: Clean up the database.

classes.name: pyln.testing.gossip.GossipStore

methods.name: pyln.testing.gossip.GossipStore.reset
documentation: Seek back to the beginning of the store.

methods.name: pyln.testing.gossip.GossipStore.__iter__
documentation: Return a copy of ourselves as iterator.
A pythonic way to interact/introspect the gossip_store

modules.name: pyln.testing.grpc2py
documentation:
Failed to index ``pyln/testing/grpc2py.py`` due to ``Syntax Error @ 2629:25.
parser error: error at 2628:24: expected NAME

        "important_plugins": [listconfigs_important_plugins2py(i) for i in m.important_plugins],  # ArrayField[composite] in generate_composite
                        ^``

functions.name: pyln.testing.fixtures.setup_logging
documentation:
Enable logging before a test, and remove all handlers afterwards.

This "fixes" the issue with pytest swapping out sys.stdout and sys.stderr
in order to capture the output, but then doesn't wait for the handlers to
terminate before closing the buffers. It just iterates through all
loggers, and removes any handlers that might be pointing at sys.stdout or
sys.stderr.

functions.name: pyln.testing.fixtures.directory
documentation:
Return a per-test specific directory.

This makes a unique test-directory even if a test is rerun multiple times.

functions.name: pyln.testing.fixtures.teardown_checks
documentation:
A simple fixture to collect errors during teardown.

We need to collect the errors and raise them as the very last step in the
fixture tree, otherwise some fixtures may not be cleaned up
correctly. Require this fixture in all other fixtures that need to either
cleanup before reporting an error or want to add an error that is to be
reported.

functions.name: pyln.testing.fixtures._extra_validator
documentation: JSON Schema validator with additions for our specialized types

functions.name: pyln.testing.fixtures._load_schema
documentation: Load the schema from @filename and create a validator for it

functions.name: pyln.testing.fixtures.jsonschemas
documentation: Load schema file if it exist: returns request/response schemas by pairs

functions.name: pyln.testing.fixtures.chainparams
documentation:
Return the chainparams for the TEST_NETWORK.

- chain_hash is in network byte order, not the RPC return order.
- example_addr doesn't belong to any node in the test (randomly generated)

functions.name: pyln.testing.utils.env
documentation:
Access to environment variables

Allows access to environment variables, falling back to config.vars (part
of Core Lightning's `./configure` output), and finally falling back to a
default value.

functions.name: pyln.testing.utils.scid_to_int
documentation: Convert a 1x2x3 scid to a raw integer

functions.name: pyln.testing.utils.only_one
documentation: Many JSON RPC calls return an array; often we only expect a single entry

functions.name: pyln.testing.utils.mine_funding_to_announce
documentation:
Mine blocks so a channel can be announced (5, if it's already
mined), but make sure we don't leave nodes behind who will reject the
announcement.  Not needed if there are only two nodes.

functions.name: pyln.testing.utils.get_tx_p2wsh_outnum
documentation: Get output number of this tx which is p2wsh of amount

functions.name: pyln.testing.utils.reserve_unused_port
documentation:
Get an unused port: avoids handing out the same port unless it's been
returned

functions.name: pyln.testing.utils.flock
documentation: A fair filelock, based on atomic fs operations.

classes.name: pyln.testing.utils.TailableProc

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.TailableProc.start
documentation:
Start the underlying process and start monitoring it. If
stdout_redir is false, you have to make sure logs go into
outputDir/log

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.TailableProc.kill
documentation: Kill process without giving it warning.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.TailableProc.cleanup_files
documentation: Ensure files are closed.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.TailableProc.logs_catchup
documentation: Save the latest stdout / stderr contents; return true if we got anything.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.TailableProc.is_in_log
documentation: Look for `regex` in the logs.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.TailableProc.is_in_stderr
documentation: Look for `regex` in stderr.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.TailableProc.wait_for_logs
documentation:
Look for `regexs` in the logs.

The logs contain tailed stdout of the process. We look for each regex
in `regexs`, starting from `logsearch_start` which normally is the
position of the last found entry of a previous wait-for logs call.
The ordering inside `regexs` doesn't matter.

We fail if the timeout is exceeded or if the underlying process
exits before all the `regexs` were found.

If timeout is None, no time-out is applied.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.TailableProc.wait_for_log
documentation:
Look for `regex` in the logs.

Convenience wrapper for the common case of only seeking a single entry.

A monitorable process that we can start, stop and tail.

This is the base class for the daemons. It allows us to directly
tail the processes and react to their output.

classes.name: pyln.testing.utils.SimpleBitcoinProxy
documentation:
Wrapper for BitcoinProxy to reconnect.

Long wait times between calls to the Bitcoin RPC could result in
`bitcoind` closing the connection, so here we just create
throwaway connections. This is easier than to reach into the RPC
library to close, reopen and reauth upon failure.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.BitcoinD.generate_block
documentation: wait_for_mempool can be used to wait for the mempool before generating blocks:
True := wait for at least 1 transation
int > 0 := wait for at least N transactions
'tx_id' := wait for one transaction id given as a string
['tx_id1', 'tx_id2'] := wait until all of the specified transaction IDs

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.BitcoinD.simple_reorg
documentation:
Reorganize chain by creating a fork at height=[height] and re-mine all mempool
transactions into [height + shift], where shift >= 0. Returns hashes of generated
blocks.

Note that tx's that become invalid at [height] (because coin maturity, locktime
etc.) are removed from mempool. The length of the new chain will be original + 1
OR original + [shift], whichever is larger.

For example: to push tx's backward from height h1 to h2 < h1, use [height]=h2.

Or to change the txindex of tx's at height h1:
1. A block at height h2 < h1 should contain a non-coinbase tx that can be pulled
   forward to h1.
2. Set [height]=h2 and [shift]= h1-h2

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.ElementsD.getnewaddress
documentation: Need to get an address and then make it unconfidential

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningD.wait
documentation:
Wait for the daemon to stop for up to timeout seconds

Returns the returncode of the process, None if the process did
not return before the timeout triggers.

classes.name: pyln.testing.utils.PrettyPrintingLightningRpc
documentation:
A version of the LightningRpc that pretty-prints calls and results.

Useful when debugging based on logs, and less painful to the
eyes. It has some overhead since we re-serialize the request and
result to json in order to pretty print it.

Also validates (optional) schemas for us.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode._create_rpc
documentation: Prepares anything related to the RPC.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.grpc
documentation: Tiny helper to return a grpc stub if grpc was configured.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.fundbalancedchannel
documentation: Creates a perfectly-balanced channel, as all things should be.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.db_manip
documentation: Assumes node is stopped!

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.stop
documentation: Attempt to do a clean shutdown, but kill if it hangs

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.restart
documentation:
Stop and restart the lightning node.

Keyword arguments:
timeout: number of seconds to wait for a shutdown
clean: whether to issue a `stop` RPC command before killing

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.subd_pid
documentation: Get the process id of the given subdaemon, eg channeld or gossipd

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.channel_state
documentation:
Return the state of the channel to the other node.

Returns None if there is no such peer, or a channel hasn't been funded
yet.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.get_channel_scid
documentation: Get the short_channel_id for the channel to the other node.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.get_channel_id
documentation: Get the channel_id for the channel to the other node.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.is_local_channel_active
documentation: Is the local channel @scid usable?

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.is_channel_active
documentation: Does gossip show this channel as enabled both ways?

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.wait_for_channel_updates
documentation: This waits until gossipd sees channel_update in both directions
(or for local channels, at least a local announcement)

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.wait_for_route
documentation: Wait for a route to the destination to become available.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.wait_for_htlcs
documentation: This helper waits for all HTLCs to settle
`scids` can be a list of strings. If unset wait on all channels.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.pay
documentation: This sends money to a directly connected peer
if `route` is `True`, it can also send over the network.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.drain
documentation: This helper sends all money to a peer until even 1 msat can't get through.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.set_feerates
documentation: Note: this feeds through the smoother in update_feerate, so changing
it on a running daemon may not give expected result!

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.force_feerates
documentation: force new feerates by restarting and thus skipping slow smoothed process
Note: testnode must be created with: opts={'may_reconnect': True}

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.wait_for_onchaind_txs
documentation: Wait for onchaind to ask lightningd to create one or more txs.  Each arg is a pair of typename, resolvename.  Returns tuples of the rawtx, txid and number of blocks delay for each pair.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.mine_txid_or_rbf
documentation: Wait for a txid to be broadcast, or an rbf.  Return the one actually mined

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.wait_for_onchaind_broadcast
documentation: Wait for onchaind to drop tx name to resolve (if any)

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.query_gossip
documentation:
Generate a gossip query, feed it into this node and get responses
in hex

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.dev_pay
documentation: Wrapper for rpc.dev_pay which suppresses the request schema

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.LightningNode.dev_invoice
documentation: Wrapper for rpc.invoice() with dev-routes option

classes.name: pyln.testing.utils.NodeFactory

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.NodeFactory.split_options
documentation:
Split node options from cli options

Some options are used to instrument the node wrapper and some are passed
to the daemon on the command line. Split them so we know where to use
them.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.NodeFactory.get_node_id
documentation: Generate a unique numeric ID for a lightning node

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.NodeFactory.get_nodes
documentation: Start a number of nodes in parallel, each with its own options

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.NodeFactory.join_nodes
documentation: Given nodes, connect them in a line, optionally funding a channel, wait_for_announce waits for channel and node announcements

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.NodeFactory.line_graph
documentation: Create nodes, connect them and optionally fund channels.

methods.name: pyln.testing.utils.NodeFactory.killall
documentation: Returns true if every node we expected to succeed actually succeeded
A factory to setup and start `lightningd` daemons.
